facade_colour: charcoal-painted wood siding with black storefront fascia and vivid blue, pink, and purple mural panels across the side parking-lot elevation

context.street_character: Major arterial | 4+ lanes | Streetcar (510) | 48m ROW | Large-scale commercial

assessment.signage: For Lease signage in lower window; graffiti tags on lower right wall

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: charcoal-painted wood siding with black storefront fascia and vivid blue, pink, and purple mural panels across the side parking-lot elevation
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: The building is active and painted, but wiring, patched surfaces, and mixed rear-lot conditions give it an improvised character.
photo_observations.window_details: Upper floor has two sash-like windows including a small projecting bay element, while the commercial level has one storefront window visible at the corner plus mural-covered wall sections.
photo_observations.door_details: One glazed commercial corner entry serves the coffee business and one solid side/service door is centered in the mural wall.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Simple eaves and fascia with no significant historic cornice expression.
photo_observations.bay_window_details: Small projecting second-floor bay with dark cladding and side panes.
photo_observations.porch_details: No porch; storefront meets the paved side lot directly.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney visible in the observed elevation.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Large custom mural artwork and the black SPECIALTY COFFEE sign are the dominant visual features.
photo_observations.roof_type_observed: Simple gabled roof over the side elevation
photo_observations.storefront_description: Corner coffee storefront with black fascia signage at the front edge and a mural-covered side wall facing the small parking area.
photo_observations.signage_observed: SPECIALTY COFFEE, @COSMOCAFEBAR, and mural artist tags along the side wall.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Extensive repainting, mural installation, updated storefront corner, and visible service wiring have altered the building from its earlier appearance.
photo_observations.notes: Observation is based on the side and corner lot-facing elevation rather than a straight-on Spadina frontage.

storefront_description: Corner coffee storefront with black fascia signage at the front edge and a mural-covered side wall facing the small parking area.
notes: Observation is based on the side and corner lot-facing elevation rather than a straight-on Spadina frontage.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 6.1 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a flat.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The visible opening pattern is organized as 1, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 2 principal entrances.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include bay window, original windows, cornice.

facade_description: 398 Spadina Ave presents a street-facing facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 6.1 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a flat. The facade reads primarily as wood clapboard in a charcoal-painted wood siding with black storefront fascia and vivid blue, pink, and purple mural panels across the side parking-lot elevation palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The visible opening pattern is organized as 1, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 2 principal entrances. Character-defining facade elements include bay window, original windows, cornice.
